name: [Unknown Item]
description: Migration fallback for unrecognized items.
_migrationNote: Replace with real ID when identified

name: Smoked Fish
description: Cellar-cured fish. Restores 4 HP. Tastes of salt and old wood.
_designNote: HP primary — use pink #FF6B9D glow under emoji

name: Kelp Wrap
description: Coastal snack. Restores 2 HP and 2 Energy. Seawater aftertaste.
_designNote: Mixed HP/Energy — use pink glow (HP primary)

name: Energy Ration
description: Military surplus energy bar. Restores 4 Energy. Tastes like chalk and ambition.
_designNote: Energy primary — use blue #00D4FF glow

name: Sailor's Rum
description: Strong stuff. Restores 6 HP but costs 2 Energy. Worth it.
_designNote: HP primary with tradeoff. Cellar biome emphasis

name: Bioluminescent Coral
description: Peculiar. Slightly electric. Restores 3 Energy and 1 HP.
_designNote: Sealab biome emphasis. Energy primary

name: Coal Cake
description: Dense foundry bread. Restores 3 HP and 1 Energy. Still warm.
_designNote: Foundry biome emphasis

name: Battery Cell
description: Standard cell. ◈ +1.
_designNote: Walk-over. Cyan #00FFA6. Dropped by construct enemies, sealab breakables

name: Power Pack
description: Surplus military power cell. ◈ +3.
_designNote: Walk-over. Found in foundry and sealab

name: Wrecker's Mallet
description: Adds +1 damage to Flame cards. Heavy, hot.
_designNote: Found in foundry breakables. Synergizes with flame deck

name: Frosted Lens
description: +1 damage on Frost cards. Ice clings to the glass.
_designNote: Found in cellar chests

name: Static Coil
description: +1 damage on Storm cards. Hums with residual charge.
_designNote: Found in sealab breakables. Wired to Storm synergy

name: Bone Hook
description: +2 damage. Carved from a dragon rib. The Tide Council pays well for these.
_designNote: Tide Council shop item. Dragon relic subtype

name: Slag Gauntlet
description: Foundry-forged. +2 Flame damage, +1 defense. Smells like burning.
_designNote: Foundry shop item

name: Pressure Suit Fragment
description: +3 defense vs Storm damage. Admiralty surplus. Mostly intact.
_designNote: Admiralty shop item

_comment: === BUFF ITEMS (PASSIVE EQUIPMENT) ITM-036 to ITM-054 ===
_designPhilosophy: Wax on, wax off. Every maintenance buff has a hidden combat face. The player buys these for cleaning efficiency and discovers they've been training. Scrubbing IS striking. Wringing IS gripping. Dodging debris IS evasion. The shop label is the mundane verb; the combat effect reveals itself in the player's first real fight.

name: Loot Magnet
description: Loose coins and dropped items fly to you within 3 tiles. Walk past, don't stop.
_designNote: GR ITM-050 Magnet. Essential tempo item — eliminates stop-and-pick-up friction. The janitor who never breaks stride to grab a coin is the one who finishes on time.
_waxOnWaxOff: Awareness training. You learn to track scattered objects without looking down. In combat: incoming projectile awareness.

name: Cargo Sling
description: Simple shoulder strap. +1 bag slot. Cheap, immediate, gets the job done.
_designNote: Cheapest buff item (9g). Scrubber class can afford this with starting gold. Every extra slot = one more supply carried = one less trip to the shop.
_waxOnWaxOff: Load bearing. Carrying weight across your shoulder builds the frame that absorbs a punch.

name: Pack Mule Strap
description: Heavy-duty harness across both shoulders. +3 bag slots. Haul the whole dungeon in one trip.
_designNote: Mid-tier bag expansion. The 'aha' purchase — player realizes they've been making unnecessary shop trips.
_waxOnWaxOff: Endurance training. Hauling a full bag up two flights of stairs, day after day. Your legs don't burn anymore.

name: Foreman's Harness
description: Boss-tier carry rig. +4 bag slots. 15% chance your items survive when you don't.
_designNote: GR ITM-061 Tactical Harness adapted. Premium purchase — most expensive buff item. The Foreman class can afford this outright (75g start). item_save triggers on player death: 15% per slot to keep the item through respawn.
_waxOnWaxOff: The harness distributes weight across the whole body. You move differently. Balanced. Centered. When something hits you, you roll with it.

name: Elbow Grease
description: Scrub 25% faster. Break things 15% quicker. Repetition builds power.
_designNote: Gleaner original. The first item that hints at the wax-on-wax-off connection. clean_speed feeds CleaningSystem tick rate. break_speed feeds BreakableSpawner hitBreakable damage or animation.
_waxOnWaxOff: Wax on. Every scrubbing motion is a palm strike. The circular motion that cleans a bloodstain is the same rotation that deflects a blade. The player who buys this for cleaning discovers their combat swipe resolves faster too.

name: Mop Wringer
description: Cleaning tools last 30% longer. You've learned to wring every drop out.
_designNote: GR ITM-084 Bandolier → resource refund adapted to tool durability. grip_strength is a hidden stat that affects weapon card damage. The player won't see the connection until combat.
_waxOnWaxOff: Wax off. Wringing a mop a thousand times a day builds the grip that doesn't let go of a weapon. The twist that squeezes water out of cotton is the twist that breaks a wrist lock.

name: Watchman's Lamp
description: See 1 tile further in darkness. A watchman's hand-me-down. The wick still holds.
_designNote: GR ITM-094 Proximity Sensor → light radius. Cheap and immediately useful in depth-3 dungeons where FOG.DARKNESS is the rendering model. light_radius feeds into Lighting.getPlayerRadius().
_waxOnWaxOff: Working in the dark teaches you to feel the room. The lamp extends what you see; the hours in darkness extended what you sense.

name: Keen Eye
description: Spot interactable objects from 1 tile further. Crates, torches, and traps glow faintly.
_designNote: GR ITM-095 Patience Module → interact range. highlight_interactable adds a subtle pulse to BREAKABLE, TORCH_UNLIT, TRAP tiles within extended range. Helps new players find what needs doing.
_waxOnWaxOff: A janitor who checks every corner for dust learns to read a room. In combat: you see the enemy's tells before they commit.

name: Fuel Pouch
description: 25% chance torch fuel isn't consumed when filling a slot. Stretch your oil.
_designNote: GR ITM-080 Surge Protector battery_save → torch fuel save. Same probability mechanic, different resource. Particularly valuable at depth-3 where torch score is weighted low but fuel is scarce.
_waxOnWaxOff: Conservation is discipline. Measuring fuel, wasting nothing. In combat: every card played with precision, no wasted energy.

name: Steady Hand
description: Rearm traps 50% faster. Your hands don't shake anymore.
_designNote: GR ITM-082 Recoil Dampener → trap speed. The hidden card_play_speed bonus (10% faster card resolution) is the wax-on reveal: steady hands from trap work = faster card play in combat.
_waxOnWaxOff: Resetting a hair-trigger trap mechanism without flinching. That's the same nerve it takes to play a card into a counter-attack window.

name: Rat Guard
description: Thick shin wraps. +1 defense vs vermin. You've been bitten enough.
_designNote: Gleaner original. Cheapest combat buff (9g, same as Cargo Sling and Lamp). The vermin tag restriction keeps it honest — won't help against the Vault Warden. The 5% evasion is the hidden wax-on bonus.
_waxOnWaxOff: Dodging rats in tight corridors, day after day. Your feet learned to move before your brain decided. That's evasion.

name: Thick Apron
description: Foundry-grade work apron. +2 defense. Stained with things you'd rather not identify.
_designNote: Gleaner original. The big defensive purchase. clean_splash_resist prevents blood splatter from re-dirtying the player's adjacent tiles when scrubbing. Dual nature: cleaning utility + flat combat defense.
_waxOnWaxOff: An apron that's absorbed acid, blood, slag, and hero vomit doesn't notice a sword.

name: Salvage Gloves
description: +1 item from corpse processing. 30% bonus drop from detritus. Practiced hands find more.
_designNote: Gleaner original. Corpse processing and detritus breaking both yield more items. The player who processes every body and breaks every flask builds inventory faster.
_waxOnWaxOff: Reaching into a dead hero's armor to strip salvage. Finding the hidden pocket, the concealed blade, the thing everyone else missed. In combat: you read the enemy's loadout.

_comment: === KEYS & FACTION PASSES ITM-060 to ITM-079 ===

name: Cellar Key
description: Opens locked cellar doors. The Tide Council distributes these.
_designNote: Dropped by cellar enemies. Auto-consume at locked doors

name: Foundry Pass
description: Stamped metal pass. Access to inner foundry areas.
_designNote: Dropped by foundry construct enemies

name: Sealab Keycard
description: Magnetic strip. Grants access to Admiralty research sections.
_designNote: Dropped by sealab marines and constructs

name: Hero's Dogtag
description: Found deep in the dungeons. Every faction wants this for different reasons.
_designNote: Tier 3 quest key. Boss drop. All factions offer payment

_comment: === SUPPLY CONSUMABLES (SHOP STOCK) ITM-080 to ITM-099 ===
_designNote: Junk tier. 1-8g. Always available at Lantern Row shops. Never competes with card spending. See SHOP_REFRESH_ECONOMY §6.5

name: Stale Rations
description: Day-old bread from a faction mess hall. Fills a crate slot or a stomach.
_designNote: Dual-use: eat for 2 HP or deposit in crate HP_FOOD slot

name: Dead Cell
description: Drained power cell. Useless to heroes, perfect for crate circuits.
_designNote: Pure crate fill — no direct use benefit

name: Weak Tonic
description: Watered-down stamina brew. Barely a buzz, but the crate doesn't care.
_designNote: Dual-use: drink for 1 energy or deposit in crate ENERGY slot

name: Scrap Parchment
description: Torn pages from a hero's journal. The ink's still wet in places.
_designNote: Crate fill only — SCROLL tag. Slightly pricier (heroes value paper)

name: Glass Bead
description: Cheap costume jewelry. Heroes wouldn't touch it, but a crate slot's a crate slot.
_designNote: GEM tag crate fill. Priciest fill item — gems are premium even fake

name: Generic Salvage
description: Miscellaneous dungeon debris. Fits any crate slot in a pinch.
_designNote: WILDCARD tag — fills any crate slot but no bonus for tag match

name: Torch Oil
description: Cheap lamp oil. Keeps a torch burning for one more cycle.
_designNote: Torch relighting consumable. Partial readiness credit on use

name: Water Bottle
description: Plain water. Drink it, clean with it, extinguish things. The cheapest thing in the shop.
_designNote: Cheapest supply. Triple-use: drink (1 HP), clean, or extinguish. Never wrong to buy

name: Cleaning Rag
description: Threadbare cloth. Cleans slowly but it's a gold piece. What do you want.
_designNote: Slowest clean tool, cheapest. 3 uses before worn out

name: Mop Head
description: Replacement head for a standard-issue mop. Gets the job done.
_designNote: Mid-tier clean tool. 5 uses, standard speed

name: Scrub Brush
description: Industrial bristles. Cleans fast and lasts. The most expensive supply and worth every coin.
_designNote: Best clean tool. 8 uses, fast speed. Price ceiling of supply tier (8g)

name: Bone Powder
description: Ground calcium. Speeds up corpse dissolution. Don't ask whose bones.
_designNote: Corpse processing ingredient — consume to process one corpse faster

name: Trap Spring
description: Spare mechanism for dungeon traps. Click it into place and walk away.
_designNote: Trap rearming consumable. Future expansion: different spring types for different trap tiers

_comment: === SALVAGE PARTS — defined in salvage.js, listed here for reference ===

name: Dragon Scale
description: A shed scale from a dragon that passed through. Iridescent. Hot to the touch.
_designNote: Generated by Salvage module. Listed here for registry completeness

name: Monster Bone
description: Thick and dense. Various monsters leave these behind.
_designNote: Generated by Salvage module

name: Vital Organ
description: Still warm. The Admiralty pays premium for intact specimens.
_designNote: Generated by Salvage module

name: Metal Scrap
description: Industrial remnants. The Foundry buys it by the weight.
_designNote: Generated by Salvage module

name: Data Crystal
description: Records something. The Admiralty won't say what.
_designNote: Generated by Salvage module

name: Monster Ichor
description: Viscous. Bioluminescent in the dark. Handle carefully.
_designNote: Generated by Salvage module

name: Tough Hide
description: Thick leathery hide. Good for lining or trade.
_designNote: Generated by Salvage module

name: Dragon Ember
description: The Hero dropped these. Still burning inside.
_designNote: Generated by Salvage module. Tide Council 2x (dragon tag)

_comment: === DETRITUS DROPS (BREAKABLE LOOT) ITM-110 to ITM-119 ===
_designNote: Items that drop from adventurer detritus breakables. Primary purpose: crate filling. See DEPTH3_CLEANING_LOOP_BALANCE §2.2

name: Potion Residue
description: Congealed potion dregs scraped from a cracked flask. The crate wants it.
_designNote: Drops from Cracked Flask detritus. HP_FOOD crate fill

name: Scrap Metal
description: Bent steel from a hero's battered shield. Universal crate fill.
_designNote: Drops from Dented Shield detritus. WILDCARD crate fill

name: Wood Scrap
description: Splintered arrow shafts. Firewood-grade lumber for crate slots.
_designNote: Drops from Broken Arrow Bundle detritus. ENERGY crate fill

name: Torn Cloth Strip
description: Ripped from a hero's satchel. Fibers still hold a little magic.
_designNote: Drops from Torn Satchel detritus. SCROLL crate fill (cloth ≈ fiber ≈ parchment)

name: Hero's Leftovers
description: Half-eaten rations a hero couldn't finish. Still nutritious. Mostly.
_designNote: Drops from Hero's Discarded Rations detritus. Dual-use: eat for 1 HP or HP_FOOD crate fill
